svg.text: Alternative base prices per item — for employee meals, VIP members, happy hour pricing, wholesale accounts. Distinct from discounts — shows on reports as the actual price, not a deduction.
Price levels change the BASE PRICE shown on the ticket and receipt. Discounts are deductions from base price.
Use price levels for recurring pricing structures (employee meal, VIP tier, wholesale); use discounts for one-time promotions.
Applies to all orders unless overridden — this is the base menu price
Triggered by: Staff clock-in → auto-apply during shift
Price adjustment: 50% off base price (multiplier 0.5) · Full tax applies
Applies to: All items · Daily limit: $15 per employee per shift
Price adjustment: 20% off base price for beverages (multiplier 0.8)
Applies to: Beverages category only · Linked to menu schedule
Triggered by: Customer loyalty tier = Gold OR VIP tag
Triggered by: House account assignment OR manager override
Price adjustment: Custom per item (use price matrix)
When multiple levels apply, higher priority wins. Drag to reorder.
Wholesale column cells are editable (blue border). All other columns show auto-calculated prices.
IS the price, not a deduction)
Happy Hour: auto-switches 4 PM via menu schedule · Employee daily limit enforced server-side ($15/shift)
pos_price_levels: id, name_i18n jsonb, multiplier decimal, trigger_type varchar,
pos_menu_item_prices: item_id × price_level_id → override_price_cents (wholesale custom prices)
pos_employee_meal_log: date + staff_id + total_cents (daily limit enforcement)
apply_price_level() evaluates triggers in priority order → sets pos_order_items.price_level_id + actual_price_cents
Reports use actual_price_cents (revenue at price level prices, not base price). Employee daily limit checked via meal log.
Inline Wholesale edits → pos_menu_item_prices upsert. Multiplier-based levels computed at order time, not stored per-item.
